author: @kngofcmx03
text:
This is why I’m totally skeptical of everything I see in movies now. They are not making movies to entertain the mass audience who just want to see a good movie, whether it’s a love story, a sci-fi heroes journey, or a basic action flick. 
They are making movies, that are completely devoid of any substance, story, or character development for us to care about. Or they ruin their own movies because they can’t help themselves to inject their political and idealisms into a movie, where it absolutely has no place for it. And then once it bombs at the box office, it usually us who are to blame, us who are the villains because we didn’t go see their trash movie, or we have our critical opinions about it. The majority audience. 
Well. One of them said a long time ago, “if you don’t like my political views or care about creative expression. Then don’t watch it.”
And I haven’t, for a long time. You want to appeal to idiots who are into being lectured and into that type of horseshit, go for it. Your true goals are to appeal to that audience. And you don’t even know, they don’t even exist.

author: @littlejimmy7402
text:
The original "Bride of Frankenstein" was always a well-made movie, but it was a sequel.  The story isn't really very complicated at all, the excitement for a sequel had more to do with the overall power of the movie at the time.  The make-up, lighting, sets,  and effects were the draw, the story is semi-incidental.  

Re-making 'Bride' as a stand alone movie is odd because the original film clicks right in at the end of "Frankenstein", where does this movie fit?  Why even make it?
